options.label: A
options.text: A force that pushes objects away from each other

options.label: B
options.text: A force that attracts objects towards each other

options.label: C
options.text: A type of energy found only in stars

options.label: D
options.text: The speed at which planets move around the Sun

correct_answer: A force that attracts objects towards each other
explanation: Gravity is an attractive force that pulls objects with mass towards each other. Although all objects attract each other through gravity, we only notice this force with very massive objects like planets, stars, and moons. For example, gravity is what keeps you on Earth and what keeps the Moon orbiting around Earth.
